questions.q: Who was the emperor of Magadha whom Bhima slew in a wrestling duel?
questions.explanation: Jarasandha, the mighty king of Magadha, was torn apart by Bhima after a marathon wrestling match.

questions.q: From which capital city, ringed by hills, did Jarasandha rule Magadha?
questions.explanation: Girivraja, protected by its famous ring of five hills, was the fortress capital of Magadha.

questions.q: Which Pandava fought and killed Jarasandha?
questions.explanation: Jarasandha chose Bhima as the worthiest opponent, and Bhima tore him in two.

questions.q: In what disguise did Krishna, Bhima, and Arjuna enter Jarasandha's capital?
questions.explanation: The three entered Girivraja dressed as snataka brahmins, graduates observing a religious vow.

questions.q: Who joined the two halves of the infant Jarasandha into one living body?
questions.explanation: The demoness Jara fitted the discarded halves together, and the joined child was named Jarasandha after her.

questions.q: To which god did Jarasandha intend to sacrifice his captured kings?
questions.explanation: Jarasandha planned to offer a hundred captured kings in sacrifice to Shiva, also called Rudra or Mahadeva.

questions.q: How many hills famously ringed the city of Girivraja?
questions.explanation: Girivraja was guarded by five hills, which made the fortress city nearly unassailable.

questions.q: Why did Krishna insist that Jarasandha be defeated before Yudhishthira's Rajasuya?
options: He had stolen the sacrificial horse
He held captive kings and blocked any claim to empire
He had insulted Draupadi at her swayamvara
He had seized the treasury of Indraprastha
questions.explanation: No one could claim imperial status while Jarasandha caged scores of kings and overshadowed all rivals.

questions.q: Besides Krishna and Bhima, who was the third member of the party that went to Girivraja?
questions.explanation: Krishna took Bhima and Arjuna with him, leaving Yudhishthira to prepare the sacrifice.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata's account, in what form of combat did Jarasandha face Bhima?
questions.explanation: In the epic the two giants met in an unarmed wrestling match, Jarasandha's proudest skill; the Bhagavata Purana retelling adds an opening duel with maces.

questions.q: Which sage's gift of a single fruit, shared between two queens, led to Jarasandha's birth in two halves?
questions.explanation: King Brihadratha split the sage Chandakaushika's fruit between his two wives, and each bore half a child.

questions.q: Jarasandha's daughters Asti and Prapti were married to which ruler?
questions.explanation: Both daughters were wives of Kamsa, which made Krishna, Kamsa's slayer, Jarasandha's sworn enemy.

questions.q: How did Jarasandha respond to Krishna's killing of his son-in-law Kamsa?
options: He sued for peace and alliance
He attacked Mathura again and again
questions.explanation: Burning to avenge Kamsa, Jarasandha launched repeated sieges against Krishna's city of Mathura.

questions.q: What did Jarasandha's relentless campaigns finally force Krishna to do?
options: Move the Yadavas to the coastal fortress of Dwarka
Take refuge in the Khandava forest
questions.explanation: To spare his people endless war, Krishna shifted the Yadavas from Mathura to the new sea fortress of Dwarka.

questions.q: How many kings was Jarasandha holding captive when Krishna's party arrived?
questions.explanation: He had imprisoned eighty-six kings and meant to complete a full hundred before the great sacrifice.

questions.q: Who was placed on Magadha's throne after Jarasandha's death?
questions.explanation: Krishna crowned Jarasandha's son Sahadeva, who became an ally of the Pandavas.

questions.q: Which landmark did the disguised trio break on their way into Girivraja?
questions.explanation: They smashed the revered Chaityaka peak and entered the city by force rather than through its gates.

questions.q: For roughly how long is Bhima's wrestling match with Jarasandha said to have raged?
questions.explanation: The epic describes them grappling without pause for some thirteen days, with Jarasandha slain on the fourteenth, though retellings vary the count.

questions.q: How did Krishna show Bhima the way to kill an opponent whose body kept rejoining itself?
options: He hurled his discus as a sign
He split a blade of grass and tossed the halves in opposite directions
He whispered a mantra of unbinding
He drew two crossing lines in the dust
questions.explanation: Krishna's silent hint with the split blade of grass taught Bhima to fling the halves apart so they could not rejoin.

questions.q: When Krishna revealed their identities and demanded he free the captive kings or fight, what did Jarasandha do?
options: He released half the kings as ransom
He offered his kingdom for peace
He refused release and chose Bhima as his opponent
He summoned his army to seize the three
questions.explanation: Proud Jarasandha refused to free the kings and, offered single combat with any of the three, picked Bhima as his equal.

questions.q: Who was Jarasandha's father, the king of Magadha before him?
questions.explanation: The childless King Brihadratha received the fateful fruit from sage Chandakaushika and fathered Jarasandha.

questions.q: With which modern town is Girivraja, Jarasandha's capital, identified?
questions.explanation: Girivraja, later called Rajagriha, survives as modern Rajgir in Bihar, still ringed by its hills.

questions.q: Why did the demoness Jara unite and return the two halves of the infant instead of devouring them?
options: A mantra of the sage compelled her to mercy
The infant's golden aura paralyzed her hunger
Brihadratha had once spared her forest kin
She was the palace's honored household goddess and favored the king
questions.explanation: Jara was worshipped as the household deity of Brihadratha's palace, so she returned the joined child to the king out of goodwill.

questions.q: Which two generals of Jarasandha, already dead before Krishna's mission, had once made his armies nearly unstoppable?
questions.explanation: Krishna noted that with Hamsa and Dimbhaka gone, Jarasandha could at last be defeated in single combat.

questions.q: What made Jarasandha suspect his three brahmin guests were no true snatakas?
options: They refused to speak in Sanskrit
They wore garlands and entered by breaching the wall rather than the gate
They carried hidden swords beneath their robes
They would not bless his sacrificial fire
questions.explanation: Their flower garlands, unguents, bowstring-scarred arms, and forced entry betrayed warriors, and Krishna replied that enemies do not enter by the door.

questions.q: After tearing Jarasandha in two, how did Bhima prevent the halves from rejoining?
options: He flung the halves in opposite directions
He burned the halves on separate pyres
He buried each half beneath a hill
He crushed the halves beneath a boulder
questions.explanation: Following Krishna's hint, Bhima threw each half away from the other so the body joined at birth could never knit again.

questions.q: What part did Jarasandha's son Sahadeva of Magadha later play in the great war?
options: He stayed neutral within his fortress
He led an akshauhini for Duryodhana
He fought on the Pandava side
He guarded the Yadava lands at Dwarka
questions.explanation: Grateful for his crown, Sahadeva of Magadha brought his forces to Kurukshetra on the side of the Pandavas.

questions.q: From what were Girivraja's great drums, whose single beat was said to resound for a month, fashioned?
options: The hide of the demon Rishabha
questions.explanation: Three great drums made from the slain demon Rishabha's hide guarded the city, and the intruders destroyed them.

questions.q: How many times is Jarasandha commonly said to have led his armies against Mathura?
questions.explanation: Tradition counts seventeen sieges of Mathura, with some retellings adding an eighteenth campaign before Krishna withdrew to Dwarka.

questions.q: By what dynastic name is Jarasandha's royal house of Magadha known?
questions.explanation: The dynasty took the name Barhadratha from Jarasandha's father Brihadratha, its founder.
